concepts.name: Graph Representation
concepts.short_definition: Methods for storing graph data, such as lists of neighbors or a matrix of connections.

concepts.name: Weighted Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where each edge has an associated numerical value.

concepts.name: Negative Edge Weights
concepts.short_definition: Edges in a weighted graph that have a negative numerical value.

concepts.name: Shortest Path
concepts.short_definition: The path between two vertices in a graph with the smallest total edge weight.

concepts.name: Single Source Shortest Path
concepts.short_definition: Finding the shortest paths from a single starting vertex to all other vertices in the graph.

concepts.name: Relaxation
concepts.short_definition: The process of updating the estimated shortest distance to a vertex if a shorter path is found.

concepts.name: Bellman-Ford Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm that finds the shortest paths from a single source vertex to all other vertices in a weighted graph, even with negative edge weights.

concepts.name: Negative Cycle Detection
concepts.short_definition: Identifying if a graph contains a cycle where the sum of edge weights is negative.

concepts.name: Dynamic Programming
concepts.short_definition: An algorithmic technique that solves problems by breaking them down into smaller overlapping subproblems.

concepts.name: Iterative Improvement
concepts.short_definition: A general approach where a solution is progressively improved through repeated steps.
